{
  "task_echo": "Choose between Electron and an `aiki serve` interface, refine the strongest hackathon concept, pressure-test adoption, and define standout features plus a practical solo development plan. The recommendation must preserve Aiki's local-first, no-API-key orchestration of installed provider CLIs.",
  "strongest_version": "Build `aiki serve` as a local Decision Studio for developers who already use Claude Code, Codex, or Gemini/Antigravity. A developer starts one command, submits an idea or code-review question, and watches a structured council expose agreements, conflicts, evidence, and judge decisions before producing a traceable synthesis. This wins if the interface demonstrates why multiple models produced a better decision rather than merely displaying several chat streams. Shipping inside the existing npm package minimizes adoption and demo friction, while a replayable capsule preserves the demonstration when provider authentication or connectivity fails. The narrow wedge should remain idea stress-testing and code review—not generic research—because those workflows match Aiki's published positioning and give judges a concrete before-and-after comparison.",

  "deliverable_proposals": [
    {
      "output": "FEATURE_BACKLOG",
      "title": "Council Arena",
      "detail": "Stream each provider as a distinct council member with status, structured claim cards, agreements, contradictions, and judge selections. Show concise model-produced rationales rather than hidden chain-of-thought.",
      "user_value": "Developers can understand where models disagree and why the final answer is stronger without reading three complete transcripts.",
      "why_distinctive": "It turns orchestration into an inspectable decision process instead of presenting an ordinary multi-column chat interface.",
      "evidence_ids": ["E1", "E2"]
    },
    {
      "output": "FEATURE_BACKLOG",
      "title": "Verdict Provenance",
      "detail": "Attach every major final recommendation to the supporting council claims and show rejected or unresolved dissent beside it.",
      "user_value": "Users can audit the synthesis, investigate weak assumptions, and avoid treating the final answer as unexplained consensus.",
      "why_distinctive": "The memorable output becomes a defensible decision artifact rather than another generated document.",
      "evidence_ids": ["E1", "E2"]
    },
    {
      "output": "FEATURE_BACKLOG",
      "title": "Challenge the Verdict",
      "detail": "Let the user select one final claim, ask the strongest dissenting model to attack it, and run one bounded re-synthesis that highlights exactly what changed.",
      "user_value": "Developers can pressure-test the most consequential assumption without restarting an entire research session.",
      "why_distinctive": "It demonstrates that the council is interactive and falsification-oriented, not merely parallel generation.",
      "evidence_ids": ["E1", "E2"]
    },
    {
      "output": "FEATURE_BACKLOG",
      "title": "Replayable Run Capsule",
      "detail": "Persist a completed real run as a local event sequence that can be replayed with a visible Recorded Run badge, provider metadata, timestamps, and final artifact.",
      "user_value": "Users can revisit, share locally, and compare past decisions; the demo remains reliable if a provider is unavailable.",
      "why_distinctive": "The same feature solves genuine reproducibility and hackathon-stage reliability without faking live execution.",
      "evidence_ids": ["E1", "E4"]
    },
    {
      "output": "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN",
      "title": "Day 1: Thin Serve Boundary",
      "detail": "Add `aiki serve` to the existing package, bind only to loopback, launch a browser with an unguessable session token, serve one static application, and adapt the existing orchestrator into validated run events. Permit only allowlisted read-only Aiki workflows.",
      "user_value": "One command opens a safe local interface while preserving current CLI behavior and provider access.",
      "why_distinctive": "It reuses Aiki's existing execution engine instead of building a second Electron runtime or another orchestration layer.",
      "evidence_ids": ["E1", "E2", "E3"]
    },
    {
      "output": "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN",
      "title": "Days 2–3: One Complete Decision Workflow",
      "detail": "Implement provider preflight, prompt submission, run creation, parallel status, validated event streaming, cancellation, judging, synthesis, and local artifact loading for one idea stress-test template. Verify the full path with all three providers before adding visual extras.",
      "user_value": "The user can complete the core job end to end and receive a real synthesized decision.",
      "why_distinctive": "A single working council proves the product thesis more effectively than many disconnected screens.",
      "evidence_ids": ["E1", "E2", "E3"]
    },
    {
      "output": "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN",
      "title": "Days 4–5: Build the Demonstration Layer",
      "detail": "Add Council Arena, Verdict Provenance, a bounded Challenge the Verdict interaction, responsive visual polish, empty/error states, and recorded-run replay. Cut Challenge the Verdict first if the core run is unstable.",
      "user_value": "The orchestration becomes understandable, memorable, and usable during both live and recorded demonstrations.",
      "why_distinctive": "The schedule prioritizes the visual evidence of multi-model value while preserving a clear cut line.",
      "evidence_ids": ["E2", "E3", "E4"]
    },
    {
      "output": "IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN",
      "title": "Day 6: Demo and Adoption Hardening",
      "detail": "Test from a clean installation, verify provider-missing and provider-failure paths, record one excellent real run, prepare a three-minute narrative comparing single-model output with Aiki's verdict, and run five target-developer evaluations.",
      "user_value": "Judges see a reliable demonstration and the submission includes early evidence that developers value the result.",
      "why_distinctive": "It makes comparative output quality—not interface spectacle alone—the closing proof.",
      "evidence_ids": ["E3", "E4", "E5"]
